cross_scene_arcs.title: From Absence to Reckoning: Alexandra's Return and Emotional Reengagement
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Alexandra transitions from physical and emotional absence to active confrontation with her fractured family, catalyzed by her mother's illness. Her return initiates a painful reconnection, shifting her role from estranged daughter to truth-bearer and emotional catalyst.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Alexandra remains absent, symbolically present only in Matt's hope for reconciliation
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Alexandra shatters family denial by revealing her mother's infidelity, assuming emotional responsibility in the family
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The broader family has not yet processed the revelations, and Alexandra's own emotional wounds remain unhealed

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Truth-Seeking Mission: Alexandra as Investigator and Moral Agent
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Alexandra evolves from passive knowledge of her mother's affair to leading an active investigation, asserting agency in uncovering family secrets. Her pursuit of truth becomes a central mission, aligning her with Matt while challenging his emotional boundaries.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Alexandra holds vague knowledge of her mother's infidelity but has not acted on it
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She becomes a strategic partner in confronting Brian Speer and advocating for transparency with Scottie
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The full truth of the affair and its emotional consequences remain incomplete, and the impact on family dynamics is still unfolding

cross_scene_arcs.title: Shifting Power: Alexandra's Defiance and Assertion of Autonomy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Alexandra increasingly challenges Matt's authority and patriarchal control, asserting her right to emotional support, information, and moral judgment. Her defiance marks a growing generational and emotional rift, even as it strengthens her role within the family.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Alexandra begins to voice frustration over being misunderstood and unacknowledged
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She openly defies Matt's boundaries, controls narrative access, and imposes her values on Scottie
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The conflict between parental authority and adolescent autonomy remains tense, with no resolution on how power will be shared in the post-crisis family

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Grief to Caretaking: Alexandra's Emotional Maturation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Alexandra undergoes a transformation from emotionally guarded and reactive to a stabilizing, nurturing presence. As she processes her grief, she assumes a caretaking role, particularly for Scottie, signaling her emergence as an emotional anchor in the family.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Alexandra shares vulnerable memories of maternal absence, beginning emotional opening
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She actively comforts Scottie and reintegrates into the family through quiet, intimate presence
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her own grief remains beneath the surface, unprocessed and potentially deferred in favor of supporting others

cross_scene_arcs.title: Ambivalence and Identity: Alexandra's Struggle with Self and Legacy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Alexandra grapples with her identity amid familial collapse, torn between rebellion and responsibility, autonomy and belonging. Her journey reflects a complex negotiation of personal choices, maternal legacy, and her place in a family defined by secrets and loss.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Alexandra expresses ambivalence about drug use and feels marginalized within the extended family
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She reclaims a place in the family while still carrying unresolved internal conflict
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her long-term emotional stability and sense of self remain uncertain, especially in the aftermath of her mother's death and the family's transformation

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Denial to Acceptance: Confronting Elizabeth's Condition
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Matt transitions from emotional avoidance to full acceptance of his wife Elizabeth's irreversible condition and eventual death, marking a profound shift in their relationship from caretaking to mourning and closure.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Matt avoids emotional engagement, clinging to normalcy and hope for recovery.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Matt honors Elizabeth's memory by scattering her ashes and embracing his role as a widower and devoted father.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The complexity of Matt's lingering love and disappointment suggests incomplete emotional reconciliation with the end of his marriage.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Uncovering the Affair: The Collapse of Marital Illusion
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Matt's discovery and investigation of Elizabeth's affair with Brian Speer shatter his perception of their marriage, driving him from shock and denial to active confrontation and painful truth-seeking.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Matt is unaware of any infidelity and strives to maintain a dignified family front.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Matt forces Brian to reveal the emotional depth of the affair, accepting the betrayal as real and transformative.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Matt is left with unresolved grief over whether Elizabeth ever truly loved him, and the authenticity of their life together remains in question.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fatherhood Reclaimed: Repairing Bonds with His Daughters
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Matt evolves from an emotionally distant father to a present and engaged parent, rebuilding trust and intimacy with Scottie and Alexandra through shared crisis and vulnerability.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Matt struggles to connect with his rebellious daughters and feels overwhelmed by his inability to lead them.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Matt joins his daughters under the quilt in a moment of quiet unity, symbolizing restored familial closeness and emotional availability.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Alexandra's unresolved anger toward her mother and Scottie's emotional detachment suggest lingering tensions that may resurface.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Legacy and Identity: Rejecting Ancestral Burden
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Matt grapples with his role as trustee of the family land, ultimately rejecting financial gain in favor of cultural stewardship, signaling his break from ancestral expectations and assertion of personal values.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Matt desires to escape the weight of his family's legacy and become 'a normal guy'.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Matt refuses to sign the land sale, choosing moral responsibility over wealth and affirming a new identity rooted in integrity and connection.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The financial and legal future of the trust remains uncertain, with potential conflict from dissenting cousins looming.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Grief to Agency: The Journey of Confrontation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Matt moves from passive awareness of his wife's affair to active confrontation with Brian Speer, navigating moral ambiguity, emotional pain, and paternal authority in a journey that redefines his sense of justice and self.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Matt seeks only to understand the truth, hesitating to act on his anger.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Matt asserts control by forcing Brian to confront the affair, transforming from a victim of circumstance into an agent of emotional reckoning.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Brian's true emotional response and future actions remain unknown, leaving the broader impact of the confrontation uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Assertion of Artistic Identity vs. Institutional and Parental Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Scottie's attempt to assert her identity as a photographer through a personal art project leads to institutional rejection and escalating conflict with her father, culminating in her defiance of his authority when she refuses to disclose her private message to her mother.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Scottie is emotionally distressed and uses photography to process her mother's illness.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Scottie resists paternal control by protecting the privacy of her emotional expression, asserting her autonomy despite punishment.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The tension between Scottie's need for creative and emotional self-expression and the adults' attempts to regulate or suppress it remains unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Emotional Withdrawal to Tentative Engagement
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Scottie evolves from resisting emotional connection with her comatose mother to gradually re-engaging with her family, culminating in active participation in the mission to reunite the family during the crisis.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Scottie avoids emotional labor and resists talking or writing to her mother.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Scottie agrees to help bring her sister home and re-joins the family in a shared emotional journey.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Scottie's fear of her mother's death persists, and her deeper emotional processing remains incomplete.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Search for Belonging and Social Redemption
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Scottie's desire to reconnect with Reina forces her to confront her past actions and the betrayal by Lani, revealing how her bullying behavior stems from prior social humiliation and a need for acceptance.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Scottie is isolated and desperate to restore her friendship with Reina.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Scottie complies with her father's demand to apologize but does so insincerely, maintaining emotional distance from true accountability.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Scottie's internal resentment and need for social validation remain unaddressed, leaving her vulnerable to recurring relational conflicts.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Emergence of Emotional Maturity Amid Family Crisis
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Scottie transitions from childish curiosity and emotional detachment to asking profound questions about love, memory, and death, reflecting growing emotional awareness and a deepening connection to her mother's humanity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Scottie focuses on factual knowledge and external signs of recovery, avoiding emotional engagement.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Scottie confronts the reality of her mother's impending death and begins to process grief on a deeper, existential level.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Scottie's understanding of death and her mother's infidelity is still unfolding, leaving her emotional foundation fragile and incomplete.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Coping Through Rebellion and Imitation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: As family tensions mount, Scottie oscillates between childish behavior, performative rebellion, and mimicry of older siblings, using provocation and imitation as coping mechanisms to navigate her unstable emotional environment.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Scottie acts out through defiance and inappropriate behavior to gain attention or assert control.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Scottie adopts Alexandra's harsh language and mimics adult femininity, revealing her vulnerability and desire to belong to a more mature identity.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Scottie lacks a stable emotional anchor, and her identity formation remains reactive rather than self-directed.
